amendments.summary: Completed WS-J runtime emission as C3-only evidence with flag-gated session records, runtime record API, fixtures, ledger rows, and RFC.
Closed WS-F claim v2/scoring split and WS-H consumption surface evidence with terminal 1000/1000 regeneration, CT, API, TS, and doctest proof.
Refreshed J.5 atomic ledger evidence hash after final regeneration.
Stabilized P6 L5/L6/P6.6 regeneration timestamps and refreshed J.5 atomic ledger evidence hash.
Refreshed J.5 atomic ledger evidence hash after deterministic final regeneration.

BB-ER hardening campaign (bd epic bb-iy7): v2 runtime record dialect added (six closed schemas + conventions + examples); E4 API typed/paginated with packaged C4 validator and flat error envelope; effective_operation_policy compiler + flag-gated policy authority seam; lane_runtime library extraction; semantic_replay comparator pilot; gap-to-CT reconciliation. Evidence integrity work: E4 source index frozen to accepted packet scope (POST_FREEZE_FILE_PATTERNS in build_source_index.py) and Pi P5 live capture frozen to reuse-by-default (--recapture to refresh), making regenerate_evidence.py idempotent for the first time (two consecutive runs converge). Refreshed J.1-J.4 code-file pins for the spec'd W4/W6 changes and J.5 atomic ledger pin to the new stable fixed point.

Adversarial-review corrections (bd epic bb-iy7). PRECISION CORRECTION of the revision-47 idempotency statement: regenerate_evidence.py is idempotent for the atomic feature ledger (J.5-pinned artifact; two consecutive runs converge, verified repeatedly) because Pi P5 live capture is now reuse-by-default and the source index is membership-frozen. FULL-TREE byte fixation does NOT hold and is structurally unattainable today: e4_artifact_catalog.json carries a monotonic revision counter (+3 per run) embedded into claims via catalog_binding, and claim<->catalog hashes form a reference cycle; catalog, claims, node gates, readiness report, freshness manifest, and source index therefore change bytes every run by design. Tracked as a dedicated bd issue for a future amendment-gated redesign. Review fixups also landed: P5 L1 builder no longer writes into docs_tmp/phase_15/source_freezes and defers claim migration/node-gate refresh to generate_support_claims/CT; source-index freeze now covers file roots; archive-ref preservation on claim regeneration; policy compiler preserves explicit zero budgets; /v1/e4 records lane_id+runtime now 400s; claim-detail errors use the flat envelope; test hardening. J.1-J.5 evidence pins refreshed to current file state.

bb-jl5 resolved: full-tree regenerate_evidence.py byte-idempotence achieved and measured (17 artifacts including catalog, v2 claims, node gates, readiness report, freshness manifest, source index, CT outputs, matrix summaries identical across three consecutive runs; atomic feature ledger unchanged at sha256:9e37dbdd1656eec51b8ed58ff0e609da802b3b0747e65236291b08f3ec164fe2). Mechanism: (1) support-claim catalog_binding now binds the catalog's stable-subset digest (integrity.stable_entries_hash over entries classified by a total, role-keyed rule in agentic_coder_prototype/conformance/catalog_binding.py; unknown roles raise) plus the real catalog revision, breaking the claim<->catalog hash/revision cycles; (2) catalog revision increments only on stable-subset change; (3) regen DAG CT stage pinned via --zero-durations and --generated-at-utc; (4) seed_atomic_feature_ledger merge-writes so the ledger never passes through an intermediate seed-only state mid-run; (5) c4_chain validates catalog_binding strictly at CT/CLI and defers (catalog_binding_pending) in lane-builder prevalidation, which runs before re-binding. Governance corrections: an undisclosed _pin_artifact_catalog_revision hack and node-gate error-stripping introduced during implementation were removed; end-to-end tests now pin the binding invariant (mutation-probe verified). Catalog revision history: 131 (pre-bb-jl5) -> reset to 1 by a mid-campaign rebuild artifact -> content-versioned, currently 183; revision now versions stable evidence content only. Source index scope: BB_E4_FINAL_READINESS_REPORT.md excluded as a generated output (membership 534 -> 533); ledger derivation and all BB-ER pins unaffected. Adversarially reviewed: three blockers found and fixed; final refutation pass clean.

BB-NS WS-G API work touched the existing J.4 /v1/e4/records router/test files without changing the phase16 claim: refreshed progress evidence hashes for agentic_coder_prototype/api/e4/router.py and tests/test_e4_api_surface.py so the live progress checker remains current.
Refreshed phase 16 RFC runtime capture evidence hash after WS-C C11 documentation update; phase 16 status unchanged.
Refreshed stale Phase 16 progress evidence pins after Phase 17 WS-J/API evidence updates; no Phase 16 status or point changes.
Refreshed progress evidence pins after BB-CL T1 contract-language changes so live progress checker remains green without changing item status or evidence content.
Refreshed stale J.4 evidence pins after current workspace evidence files changed; required to keep phase 16 progress v1 validation green while B4 adds v2 pin-policy support.
Refreshed stale phase16 WS-J progress evidence pins after BB-CL T4 API/service changes; item statuses and point totals unchanged.
Refreshed evidence pins after WS-F dialect fallback deletion and API-surface test cleanup changed shared runtime/API files; no points or statuses changed.
Classified WS-F live source/test evidence-pin refresh as an allowed live-evidence advance, with old/new hashes and rationale recorded; no statuses or points changed.
Clarified WS-F live pin-refresh report with prior-hash sources and explicit note where prior bytes were not recovered; no statuses or points changed.

Phase16 WS-J no-status pin refresh after BB-CL source evolution: runtime emission, service, E4 router, and API-surface test paths changed in the live checkout while WS-J behavior remains covered by Phase18 G1/G2/G8 gates. Old pin hashes are retained in this amendment for recovery/audit; item statuses and points are unchanged.
Refreshed exactly 2 diff-caused Phase19 BB-AV evidence pins after the E7 239-file fixed-point regeneration; no statuses, points, or evidence paths changed.
Refreshed the single Phase16 pin changed by the P6.6 provider-scope repair and converged E4 regeneration; no statuses, points, or evidence paths changed.

Re-pin 2 evidence hashes flagged pin_stale by check_er_progress --pin-policy v2: the pinned conformance artifacts (claims v2->v4 migration, catalog v2 rebuild, node-gate/manifest refreshes) were legitimately regenerated by the BB-AV campaign (docs_tmp/phase_19, epic bb-71n). No item status or points changed.
